macro_context.market_sentiment: Cautiously mixed with a slight risk-off tone — Dow fell 280 pts for its fifth straight losing day; S&P 500 essentially flat (-0.04%) while Nasdaq edged up 0.04%. Market awaiting Magnificent Seven Q1 earnings (Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta) after the bell. Seagate surged 18% on AI demand but broader tech under pressure from OpenAI revenue concerns the prior session.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held rates steady at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, 2026 with a historic 8-4 dissent — most divided FOMC since October 1992. Committee signaled inflation remains elevated partly due to energy prices; opposition to forward guidance for further cuts is mounting. Jerome Powell likely at his final meeting as Chair; Kevin Warsh moving toward confirmation as successor, adding uncertainty to rate path.
macro_context.geopolitical: U.S. blockade of Iranian ports is driving oil prices higher, adding to inflationary pressure. Middle East conflict has disrupted energy markets with Strait of Hormuz closure risks. U.S. intervention in Venezuela and civil unrest in Iran are additional flashpoints. IMF projects 2026 global growth at only 3.1% with inflation at 4.4% under the baseline; adverse scenario cuts growth to 2.5%. Tariffs and critical-mineral competition remain active geostrategic tools affecting supply chains.
key_events: Amazon Q1 2026 earnings tonight after market close (April 29) — tariffs and AI the two key topics
Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta also reporting Q1 earnings tonight
Fed held rates at 3.5%-3.75%; historic 8-4 dissent signals policy uncertainty ahead
Oil prices rising on U.S. blockade of Iranian ports
Pershing Square Capital IPO raised $5B (at low end of range) — IPO market tepid
IMF 2026 WEO warns war darkens global growth outlook

ticker_news.headline: Amazon Q1 2026 earnings due tonight — Wall Street expects EPS $1.65, revenue $177.16B; tariffs and AI are the dominant themes
Anthropic commits to spend $100B+ on AWS over 10 years; Amazon deepens investment with $5B now and up to $20B more tied to milestones
Amazon Leo acquires Globalstar and partners with Apple to provide satellite direct-to-device services for iPhone and Apple Watch
Mizuho raises Amazon price target to street-high $325, reiterates Buy rating ahead of Q1 earnings — cites AWS as 'real AI winner of 2026'
Motley Fool prediction: Amazon stock will soar after April 29 earnings on AWS AI momentum

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Strong Buy consensus from 42-45 analysts; 42 Buys, 2 Holds. PT range: $175 low (DA Davidson downgrade Feb 6, 2026) to $325 high (Mizuho, Apr 28, 2026). Average PT ~$285-289. Oppenheimer set $275 on Apr 24, 2026. Overall consensus has held firm despite DA Davidson's February cut to $175. Mizuho's street-high $325 raise on Apr 28 reflects AWS AI thesis confidence.

competitor_activity: Google, Microsoft, and Meta all reporting Q1 earnings tonight alongside Amazon — earnings season is a direct peer-comparison moment. Microsoft perceived as an AI laggard vs AWS, benefiting Amazon's competitive narrative. Walmart ($713.2B revenue) trails Amazon ($716.9B) by less than $4B in retail — the retail gap is essentially closed. AWS Graviton CPU now displaces Intel x86 in 98% of top EC2 customers. Amazon Leo satellite broadband competing with SpaceX Starlink — already signed Delta Airlines, AT&T, Vodafone, Australia NBN, and NASA. AWS AI services reached $15B annual run rate in Q1 2026, competing head-to-head with Azure AI and Google Cloud AI.
earnings_info: Q1 2026 earnings report TODAY, April 29, 2026 after market close; earnings call at 5:30 PM ET. Analyst consensus: EPS $1.65 GAAP (range $1.61-$1.65), revenue ~$177.13-178B (~14% YoY growth). Company Q1 guidance: revenue $173.5B-$178.5B, operating income $16.5B-$21.5B. Key watch items: AWS revenue growth rate, tariff impact on retail margins, AI capex trajectory, and Anthropic partnership revenue contribution. Next quarterly report (Q2 2026) expected approximately late July 2026. Polymarket gives 96% probability tariffs will be mentioned on the call.
